cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.15)
set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
project(vigil C CXX)

find_library(PCAP_LIB pcap)

# ── C core library (shared by both targets) ──────────────────────────
add_library(vigil_core STATIC
  src/core/capture.c
  src/core/parse.c
  src/core/arena.c
  src/core/hashmap.c
  src/core/spscbuf.c
)
target_include_directories(vigil_core PUBLIC include)
target_link_libraries(vigil_core ${PCAP_LIB})
target_compile_options(vigil_core PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
if(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Debug")
  target_compile_options(vigil_core PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)
  target_link_options(vigil_core PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)
endif()

# ── Original CLI target ──────────────────────────────────────────────
add_executable(vigil src/core/main.c)
target_link_libraries(vigil vigil_core)
if(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Debug")
  target_compile_options(vigil PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)
  target_link_options(vigil PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)
endif()

# ── FTXUI (fetched at configure time) ────────────────────────────────
include(FetchContent)
FetchContent_Declare(ftxui
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/ArthurSonzogni/FTXUI
  GIT_TAG        v5.0.0
  GIT_SHALLOW    TRUE
)
FetchContent_MakeAvailable(ftxui)

#── Engine library (C++ protocol classifiers) ───────────────────────
add_library(vigil_engine STATIC
  src/engine/dns.cpp
  src/engine/http.cpp
)
target_include_directories(vigil_engine PUBLIC src)
target_link_libraries(vigil_engine vigil_core)
target_compile_options(vigil_engine PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
if(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Debug")
  target_compile_options(vigil_engine PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)
  target_link_options(vigil_engine PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)
endif()

# ── Dashboard target ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
add_executable(vigil_dashboard src/ui/dashboard/main.cpp)
target_link_libraries(vigil_dashboard vigil_core vigil_engine ftxui::screen ftxui::dom ftxui::component pthread)
target_compile_options(vigil_dashboard PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
if(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Debug")
  target_compile_options(vigil_dashboard PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)
  target_link_options(vigil_dashboard PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)

To achieve maximum readability on a `#000000` background while using **Pastel Pink** as the primary driver, we need to solve the "Pink-on-Black" legibility problem. 

Standard pastels can sometimes "shimmer" or appear blurry against pure black. To fix this, I have curated a **Tri-Tone Pink Scale**: a "Hot" Pink for logic, a "Pastel" Pink for data, and a "Deep" Pink for background elements. This creates a clear hierarchy so your eyes don't get fatigued by a single shade of pink.

### 1. The "Bioluminescent Rose" Palette (Source of Truth)

| Role | Hex Code | Visual Style | Readability Logic |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Background** | `#000000` | **Deep Void** | Absolute contrast for OLED/High-End monitors. |
| **Primary (Keywords)** | `#ff8fb1` | **Vivid Pink** | High saturation to ensure logic stands out. |
| **Secondary (Data)** | `#ffcfdf` | **Pastel Rose** | Brighter/Whiter for Strings/Numbers (Easy to read). |
| **Tertiary (Text)** | `#e0def4` | **Lender White** | Soft lavender-white for general variables. |
| **Muted (Comments)** | `#6e6a86` | **Dusky Purple** | Low contrast so they don't distract from code. |
| **Accent (Search)** | `#31748f` | **Steel Blue** | The only "Cool" color to make highlights pop. |

---

### 2. Neovim Configuration (`init.lua`)
This is your **Source of Truth**. It uses the "Vivid Pink" for the most important parts of the code (Keywords/Functions) and the "Pastel Rose" for literals.

```lua
local colors = {
  bg      = "#000000",
  fg      = "#e0def4", -- Main text (Lavender-White)
  pink_v  = "#ff8fb1", -- Vivid Pink (Keywords/Logic)
  pink_p  = "#ffcfdf", -- Pastel Rose (Strings/Numbers)
  iris    = "#c4a7e7", -- Pale Purple (Types/Classes)
  comment = "#6e6a86", -- Muted (Comments)
  surface = "#191724", -- Subtle UI (Gutter)
}

local hl = vim.api.nvim_set_hl

-- Editor UI
hl(0, "Normal", { fg = colors.fg, bg = colors.bg })
hl(0, "LineNr", { fg = colors.comment })
hl(0, "CursorLine", { bg = "#0d0d0f" })
hl(0, "Visual", { bg = "#403d52" })

-- Syntax (Maximizing Readability)
hl(0, "Keyword", { fg = colors.pink_v, bold = true }) -- Logic is BOLD Pink
hl(0, "Function", { fg = colors.pink_v })             -- Actions are Pink
hl(0, "String", { fg = colors.pink_p })               -- Data is Pastel
hl(0, "Number", { fg = colors.pink_p })               -- Numbers are Pastel
hl(0, "Type", { fg = colors.iris })                   -- Types are Lavender
hl(0, "Identifier", { fg = colors.fg })               -- Variables are White
hl(0, "Comment", { fg = colors.comment, italic = true })

-- Search/Match (High Contrast)
hl(0, "Search", { fg = colors.bg, bg = "#ebbcba" })   -- Peach highlight
```

### 4. Fish Shell Tweaks
Since Neovim is your source of truth, run these commands in your terminal to set your **Fish** syntax highlighting to match:

```fish
set -U fish_color_command ff8fb1 --bold  # Commands in Vivid Pink
set -U fish_color_param e0def4           # Parameters in Soft White
set -U fish_color_quote ffcfdf           # Quotes in Pastel Rose
set -U fish_color_error eb6f92           # Errors in Love Red
set -U fish_color_comment 6e6a86         # Comments in Muted Purple
```

### Why this is "Maximally Readable":
1.  **Semantic Separation:** You aren't just using "one pink." Your brain will learn that **Vivid Pink = Logic** and **Pastel Pink = Data**. This reduces the "mental tax" of reading code.
2.  **Luminance Balance:** Pure white text on pure black can cause "halation" (a glowing blur). By using a soft lavender-white (`#e0def4`) for the bulk of your text, we protect your eyes while letting the pink accents do the heavy lifting.
3.  **Black is Absolute:** By keeping `#000000` as the base for everything, your UI elements (Gutter, Statusline) feel like they are floating in the "Wired," just like in the *Lain* reference image.

How does this saturation level feel to you? If you want it even "punchier," we can move the Keywords closer to a neon magenta.

To implement this, you are effectively using a high-contrast, duotone **Black & Rose** theme. The extreme separation between pure black (`#000000`) and the desaturated pastel pinks will ensure maximum readability.

```lua

  bg       = "#000000", -- Your pure black background
  gutter   = "#101010", -- Just enough hint of grey for the line number column
  fg       = "#ffc1da", -- Pale pink for general variables/text
  rose_main= "#fcacd3", -- Your clock pink (for logic and actions)
  rose_lite= "#ffe0ec", -- Near-white pink (for numbers/literals)
  rose_dark= "#ad8699", -- Muted desaturated pink (for comments)
  rose_pop = "#ff7fb6", -- Hot pink (for search/errors)
  ui_dim   = "#2c2c2e", -- Dark grey for borders
}

local hl = vim.api.nvim_set_hl

-- Base Editor UI
hl(0, "Normal", { fg = colors.fg, bg = colors.bg })
hl(0, "LineNr", { fg = colors.rose_dark, bg = colors.gutter }) -- Dull pink on dark grey
hl(0, "CursorLine", { bg = "#0d0d0f" }) -- Very subtle line highlight
hl(0, "Visual", { bg = "#3e3a52" })
hl(0, "StatusLine", { fg = colors.bg, bg = colors.rose_main }) -- Inverted pink/black status

-- High-Contrast Syntax for Readability
-- Primary Logic = Vivid Clock Pink (rose_main)
hl(0, "Keyword", { fg = colors.rose_main, bold = true }) 
hl(0, "Function", { fg = colors.rose_main })

-- Data & Values = Near-white Pale Pink (rose_lite)
hl(0, "Constant", { fg = colors.rose_lite })
hl(0, "String", { fg = colors.rose_lite, italic = true })
hl(0, "Number", { fg = colors.rose_lite })

-- Other Semantic Separation
hl(0, "Operator", { fg = colors.fg }) -- Use the general text pink
hl(0, "Variable", { fg = colors.fg })
hl(0, "Comment", { fg = colors.rose_dark, italic = true }) -- Pushes comments "back" for focus

-- Diagnostic / Active States
hl(0, "Search", { fg = colors.bg, bg = colors.rose_pop, bold = true }) -- Inverted Hot Pink
hl(0, "ErrorMsg", { fg = colors.rose_pop })
hl(0, "WarningMsg", { fg = colors.rose_main })
```
